claim: Evidence gathered by explorer and cross-checker is SUFFICIENT to determine that demand acceptance criteria are NOT MET; cross-provider model leakage protection is asymmetric, model namespace validation is missing, and tool configuration drift is untested for non-claude providers.

evidence.summary: Claude→codex model leakage protection exists (clears ai.model if contains 'claude').
evidence.why: Verified by both agents; this is one-way protection only.

evidence.summary: Claude provider passes ai.model directly without namespace validation.
evidence.why: Cross-checker confirmed that non-claude models (e.g., 'gpt-4') would pass through unchecked.

evidence.summary: Codex defaults to 'workspace-write' sandbox (permissive write mode).
evidence.why: Verified by both agents; this creates risk if boundary snapshot detection fails.

evidence.summary: Test covers claude→codex protection but NO reverse leakage scenarios.
evidence.why: Verified by both agents; missing test coverage for codex→claude, custom→claude, custom→codex.

evidence.summary: Claude tool profile normalization tests exist but no equivalent for codex/custom providers.
evidence.why: Verified by both agents; tool configuration drift is untested for non-claude providers.

risks: Acceptance criterion 'avoids cross-provider model leakage' is NOT MET due to missing reverse-direction protection.
Model namespace validation is missing in provider-adapter.ts, allowing arbitrary model strings to pass to provider invocations.
Codex sandbox default 'workspace-write' is permissive; if boundary snapshot detection fails, mutations go undetected.
Tool configuration normalization is claude-specific; drift between providers is untested and could weaken harness boundary.
missing: Model namespace validation in provider-adapter.ts for all providers.
Reverse-direction model leakage protection (codex→claude, custom→claude, custom→codex).
Sandbox default validation (codex should default to 'boundary' for evidence agents).
Tests for reverse cross-provider model leakage scenarios.
Validation that custom providers respect sandbox='boundary' setting.
Tool configuration normalization for codex and custom providers.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is consistent, well-sourced, and sufficient to determine that acceptance criteria are NOT MET. Evidence agreement is pass (no conflicts). Assumptions are properly separated from facts and not promoted. Missing items are properly reflected. The demand has one-way cross-provider protection only, missing model namespace validation, permissive codex sandbox default, and untested reverse leakage scenarios. PRD readiness is blocked until these gaps are addressed.
